name: Grade 11 Engineering — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 11 pathway for independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Engineering. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 11 knowledge and transferable skills in independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Engineering.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Engineering
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Engineering and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

units.title: Investigate: independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Engineering
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Engineering?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Engineering.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Engineering, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 11 task connected to independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Engineering.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
